rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance
rules_evaluated.evidence: No matches for banned words (honest, delve, landscape-as-verb, importantly, crucial, substrate, leverage-verb, synergize, paradigm shift)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.evidence: No instances of practice (noun), ritual, or ceremony in body prose

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography
rules_evaluated.evidence: No date references (2026-), caught-live markers, MOVED markers, or changelog in body. Maintainer block has appropriate quality annotation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match — Bootstrap voice trio (Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Register is peer-warmth and counterintuitive reframe. Example: 'The feeling, files materialising in two folders at once, four windows churning on one question, is the lesson' (Sutherland). No L&D-coach or cold-tech-only tone.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Creator's name / author-we / authorial-meta bans
rules_evaluated.evidence: No creator name, no author-we, no authorial-meta in body. 'Author' appears only in prompt instruction context, not student narration.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always 'you', never 'the student'; teacher framing stays out of body
rules_evaluated.evidence: Uses direct imperative and second-person. No 'the student' references. Trainer mechanics in maintainer blocks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
rules_evaluated.evidence: No maintainer-specific terms (self-compound, three-pattern, grill-me) leak into body. 'Synthesizer' and 'curation' are contextualized.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector
rules_evaluated.evidence: No reassurance prose ('it's OK', 'fine way', 'no penalty'). Contingency line 'if you can't get one running today... skip it' is structural option, not hedge.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy, NOT curriculum body
rules_evaluated.evidence: No defensive positioning prose. 'No vendor blogs' appears in prompt fence (line 89), not body. Body focuses on task and feeling of parallel work.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early
rules_evaluated.evidence: Term 'connector' appears in body (line 19: 'no wiki connector, no doc store reachable') without one-breath primer. While learned operationally in same exercise (line 51: 'Run the searches through Claude's connector'), the body reference comes BEFORE operational teaching and assumes student knowledge.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add one-sentence primer when 'connector' first appears in body prose. Example: 'if you can't get one running today (no wiki connector — the link to your internal tools — no doc store reachable, network locked)...' OR reorder to introduce connectors after first prompt defines them operationally.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: Same issue: 'connector' assumed in body context-setting (line 19) before operational intro in prompt (line 51). Carve-out allows terms earned same module, but earned moment comes AFTER body reference.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: See rule 14 above. Add primer, reorder, or simplify the condition.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No em-dashes found. Sentences are short and direct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks
rules_evaluated.evidence: [NOT FOUND] markers are Claude instructions, not student fill-ins.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: All four main prompts have semantic lead-ins: 'In Session X, tell this session to act as...'. Reusable prompt has lead-in: 'Once a retriever returns its first pass, paste this back...'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape
rules_evaluated.evidence: All prompts follow correct shape: lead-in, blank line, **Prompt** *(Claude Code)*, blank line, fenced block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Skills invoke by name, never by path
rules_evaluated.evidence: No skills invoked. No path-leak.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path
rules_evaluated.evidence: Reusable 'Push another round' prompt references 'the first pass'. Paths explicit: sources/wiki-retrieval.md, sources/docs-retrieval.md, sources/internet-retrieval.md.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced blocks is dead weight
rules_evaluated.evidence: No **bold** or *italic* inside fences. Bullets and numbering used appropriately.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Append-vs-integrate — name the integration mode on regeneration
rules_evaluated.evidence: Reusable prompt says 'Append; don't rewrite.' Synthesizer says 'Integrate, don't append.' Both modes named.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths (Bootstrap-specific)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Explicit subpaths throughout: sources/wiki-retrieval.md, sources/docs-retrieval.md, sources/internet-retrieval.md, memory/_synthesis-m3.md. No naked filenames.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't restate session CWD inside the prompt body
rules_evaluated.evidence: Subpaths referenced from root without restating absolute paths or redundant CWD.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module mood (multi-agent-systems.md): 'Hire three agents to search. Three more to decide. The filesystem is the meeting room.' Exercise honors this by emphasizing parallel execution feeling: 'The feeling, files materialising in two folders at once, four windows churning on one question, is the lesson' (line 13, 21-22).

summary: File passes writing-hygiene with one non-blocking REVISE on jargon-planting temporality. The term 'connector' is used in body prose (line 19) before operational teaching in prompts (line 51). While the term IS earned operationally in same exercise, the principle is to avoid jargon in lead-in text before student encounters it. Recommend adding one-sentence primer inline, reordering body prose, or simplifying the condition. All prompt blocks well-designed with correct lead-ins and fenced code. Register matches Bootstrap voice trio (Godin/Sutherland/Siilasmaa). No banned words, no author-we, no maintainer-vocab leaks, no value-prop leak, no em-dashes. Runtime-fork scaffolding (rt-code/rt-cowork divs) correctly implemented.
